name: Spiced Quince Compote
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour the water into a saucepan and heat it.
2. Rinse the quinces, then quarter them with a sharp paring knife.
3. Carefully cut out the cores of each piece.
4. Hold each piece firmly as you work to prevent it from breaking in half.
5. Slice each quarter into pieces about 1/2-inch thick.
6. Remove the peels from the slices.
7. Place the cores and peels in the pan of water; they will add flavor and body to the water due to the natural pectin in the fruit.
8. Allow the skins and cores to simmer slowly, covered, for about 20 minutes, then strain, keeping the liquid.
9. Combine the sugar, spices, orange peel, and strained liquid in a non-corroding pan.
10. Bring to a boil, stirring to dissolve the sugar.
11. Add the quinces.
12. Place a disk of parchment paper directly on the surface to prevent any exposed slices from drying out.
13. Simmer slowly until the quinces have turned pink and are slightly translucent but still firm to the touch, about 2-2 1/2 hours.
14. Transfer them to a bowl with the syrup and chill well before serving. Once poached in syrup, quinces will last for weeks in the refrigerator.
